evidence.scientific_clarity: 10x10 confusion matrix with diagonal highlighted in deep purple and off-diagonal in Blues colormap; all cells annotated with counts; axes clearly labeled True/Predicted class — brief's task fully satisfied.
evidence.typography: PDF uses Type 3 (bitmap) fonts (DejaVuSans and DejaVuSans-Bold); script never sets pdf.fonttype=42 or rcParams; default DejaVu Sans used throughout — both major typography requirements fail.
evidence.palette: Blues sequential colormap for off-diagonal is perceptually uniform and colorblind-safe; deep purple diagonal is distinct; no red/green directional misuse — minor issue is no explicit colorblind audit.
evidence.layout_density: 7x6 inch figure fits 10x10 grid well with tight_layout; colorbar sized appropriately (fraction=0.046); no overlapping labels or excessive whitespace observed in 1004x883 PNG.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes the dual-color scheme and cites per-class accuracy range (69%–92%); legend/colorbar present; title says 'counts' matching caption — minor: no data.json provenance or class count mentioned.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap) fonts, not Type 1/TrueType; text is not selectable/searchable in PDF; pdf.fonttype=42 was never set in the script.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (26K), figure.png (59K), figure.svg (68K), caption.tex (486B), and gen_figure.py (2.4K) all exist and are non-trivial — all required outputs present.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names the visualization type, explains cell semantics (i,j counts), describes the dual colormap design, and provides a specific numerical finding (69%–92% per-class accuracy range).

overall_strengths: All required output files are present and non-trivial; the figure faithfully implements the brief's 10x10 confusion matrix with dual colormap design.
Caption is specific and informative, citing a concrete numerical range (69%–92% accuracy) and clearly explaining the visual encoding.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 is never set, resulting in Type 3 bitmap fonts in the PDF — text is not selectable or editable, and DejaVu Sans (matplotlib default) is used instead of a publication-quality font.
Vector fidelity is compromised: the PDF contains rasterized Type 3 fonts, making it unsuitable for journal submission without fixing rcParams before savefig.
